formula:0012.latex: \begin{aligned}\mathbb P[Y=(y_1,\ldots,y_d)\mid X=x]&=\mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid X=x]\prod_{i=2}^{d}\mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_{1:i-1}=y_{1:i-1},X=x],\\ \mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid X=x]&=g_\theta^1(x,y_1),\\ \mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_{1:i-1}=y_{1:i-1},X=x]&=g_\theta^i(x,y_{1:i-1},y_i).\end{aligned}
formula:0012.paper_as_printed_latex: \begin{aligned}\mathbb P[Y=(y_1,\ldots,y_d)\mid X=x]&=\mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid X=x]\prod_{i=2}^{d}\mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_{0:i-1}=y_{0:i-1},X=x],\\ \mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid X=x]&=g_\theta^1(x,y_1),\\ \mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_{0:i-1}=y_{0:i-1},X=x]&=g_\theta^i(x,y_{0:i-1},y_i).\end{aligned}
evidence: The random vector is defined immediately above as Y=(Y_1,...,Y_d), with no Y_0 component.
The chain rule at factor i must condition on the already generated components Y_1 through Y_(i-1).
The printed zero-based slice is inconsistent with both the one-based vector definition and the product starting at i=2.
formula:0012.note: Corrected the undefined Y_0 prefix to the one-based preceding-coordinate slice Y_{1:i-1} throughout equation (12).

evidence: Equation (13) is the logarithm of the chain factorization in equation (12).
Because Y is indexed from 1, the i-th factor depends on y_1 through y_(i-1), not on an undefined y_0.
formula:0013.note: Applied the one-based prefix correction from equation (12) to its log-likelihood in equation (13).

formula:0014.latex: \begin{cases}\mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid Y_1\geq y_1,X=x]=\dfrac{e^{f_\theta^{1,+}(x,y_1)}}{1+e^{f_\theta^{1,+}(x,y_1)}},&y_1\geq r_1,\\ \mathbb P[Y_1=z\mid X=x]=h_\theta^{1,z}(x),&z\in\{\{y_1>0\},\{y_1=0\},\{y_1<0\}\},\\ \mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid Y_1\leq y_1,X=x]=\dfrac{e^{f_\theta^{1,-}(x,y_1)}}{1+e^{f_\theta^{1,-}(x,y_1)}},&y_1\leq r_{-1}.\end{cases}
formula:0014.note: Reconstructed the stitched positive-tail, sign-classification, and negative-tail model for the first coordinate.

formula:0015.latex: \begin{cases}\mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_i\geq y_i,Y_{1:i-1}=y_{1:i-1},X=x]=\dfrac{e^{f_\theta^{i,+}(x,y_{1:i-1},y_i)}}{1+e^{f_\theta^{i,+}(x,y_{1:i-1},y_i)}},&y_i\geq r_1,\\ \mathbb P[Y_i=z\mid Y_{1:i-1}=y_{1:i-1},X=x]=h_\theta^{i,z}(x,y_{1:i-1}),&z\in\{\{y_i>0\},\{y_i=0\},\{y_i<0\}\},\\ \mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_i\leq y_i,Y_{1:i-1}=y_{1:i-1},X=x]=\dfrac{e^{f_\theta^{i,-}(x,y_{1:i-1},y_i)}}{1+e^{f_\theta^{i,-}(x,y_{1:i-1},y_i)}},&y_i\leq r_{-1}.\end{cases}
formula:0015.paper_as_printed_latex: \begin{cases}\mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_i\geq y_i,Y_{0:i-1}=y_{0:i-1},X=x]=\dfrac{e^{f_\theta^{i,+}(x,y_{0:i-1},y_i)}}{1+e^{f_\theta^{i,+}(x,y_{0:i-1},y_i)}},&y_i\geq r_1,\\ \mathbb P[Y_i=z\mid Y_{0:i-1}=y_{0:i-1},X=x]=h_\theta^{i,z}(x,y_{0:i-1}),&z\in\{\{y_i>0\},\{y_i=0\},\{y_i<0\}\},\\ \mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_i\leq y_i,Y_{0:i-1}=y_{0:i-1},X=x]=\dfrac{e^{f_\theta^{i,-}(x,y_{0:i-1},y_i)}}{1+e^{f_\theta^{i,-}(x,y_{0:i-1},y_i)}},&y_i\leq r_{-1}.\end{cases}
evidence: This display instantiates the conditional factor g_theta^i from corrected equation (12).
All three branches must depend on the defined preceding coordinates Y_1 through Y_(i-1); Y_0 is not a component of Y.
